  • Hide All Code

Angrist & Evans (1998) — Outcome Weights & Kappa Estimators

Childbearing & Labour Supply: SUW Replication, DML & Learner Comparison (N = 3,000 subsample)

Alexander Unger

14 June 2026

Setup

Packages & Functions

# Core
library(haven)
library(AER)
library(sandwich)
library(lmtest)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
# DML smoother + balance plots
library(OutcomeWeights)
library(cobalt)
library(viridis)
library(gridExtra)
library(ggplot2)
# DoubleML learners
library(DoubleML)
library(mlr3verse)

source("functions_all.R")
cat("functions_all.R sourced.\n")
## functions_all.R sourced.

Data & Design

Setting. Angrist & Evans (1998), 1980 Census PUMS — married women aged 21–35 with two or more children. Instrument Z = samesex (first two children same sex); treatment D = morekids (more than two children); outcome Y = log family income. Controls follow the canonical AE specification: mother’s age (agem), age at first birth (agefstm), first-child-boy (sexk), second-child-boy (sex2nd), and race indicators (blackm, hispm, othracem). Estimation runs on the saved N = 3,000 stratified subsample (ae98_sub.rds); the design structure (Z/D rates, complier share, propensity surface) is preserved but point estimates are imprecise by construction and are not compared to the full-sample reproduction.

data_path <- "/Users/alexung/Desktop/master_thesis/code/"
obj       <- readRDS(file.path(data_path, "ae98_sub.rds"))
ae98_sub  <- obj$ae98_sub

# Income sample: positive income only (subsample already income-restricted upstream,
# but re-filter to be safe and consistent with the reproduction)
ae98 <- ae98_sub[!is.na(ae98_sub$incomem) & ae98_sub$incomem > 0, ]

# Outcomes in four unit shifts (translation-invariance display, analogous to
# Card's cents/dollars). Y_dol is the working outcome everywhere else.
ae98$lincomem      <- log(ae98$incomem)              # dollars
ae98$lincomem_cnt  <- log(100    * ae98$incomem)     # cents
ae98$lincomem_ths  <- log(ae98$incomem / 1000)       # thousands
ae98$lincomem_hths <- log(ae98$incomem / 100000)     # hundred-thousands

Y_dol  <- ae98$lincomem
Y_cnt  <- ae98$lincomem_cnt
Y_ths  <- ae98$lincomem_ths
Y_hths <- ae98$lincomem_hths

Z <- as.integer(ae98$samesex)
D <- as.integer(ae98$morekids)

cat(sprintf("N (income subsample) = %d\n", nrow(ae98)))
## N (income subsample) = 3000
cat(sprintf("mean(Z)=%.4f | mean(D)=%.4f | complier share=%.4f\n",
            mean(Z), mean(D),
            mean(D[Z==1]) - mean(D[Z==0])))
## mean(Z)=0.5020 | mean(D)=0.3493 | complier share=0.0545
RHS <- "agem + agefstm + sexk + sex2nd + blackm + hispm + othracem"

# Kappa design matrix — WITH intercept (for logit / CBPS)
X_kappa <- model.matrix(as.formula(paste("~", RHS)), data = ae98)

# X_df for 2SLS (no intercept column)
X_df <- ae98[, c("agem","agefstm","sexk","sex2nd","blackm","hispm","othracem")]

# DML design matrix — no intercept, attributes stripped for grf (make_X)
X_dml <- make_X(as.formula(paste("~ 0 +", RHS)), data = ae98)

cat(sprintf("Kappa: %d covariates | DML: %dx%d\n",
            ncol(X_kappa) - 1, nrow(X_dml), ncol(X_dml)))
## Kappa: 7 covariates | DML: 3000x7

1. Reproduction — SUW Table

Layout. One treatment (more than two children), one covariate specification, four outcome-unit shifts (dollars / cents / thousands / hundred-thousands). Columns are the unit shifts. Normalized estimators (τ_cb_u, τ_ml_u, τ_ml_a10) are translation invariant — all four unit columns match; unnormalized ones (τ_ml_a, τ_ml_t, τ_ml_a0) shift with the units. With the reduced sample these reproduce the structure of the original table, not its point estimates.

run_ae_column <- function(Y) {
  list(
    tsls  = run_2sls(Y, D, Z, X_df, endog_name = "morekids"),
    kappa = kappa_analytic_se_all(Y, Z, D, X_kappa)
  )
}

col_dol  <- run_ae_column(Y_dol)
col_cnt  <- run_ae_column(Y_cnt)
col_ths  <- run_ae_column(Y_ths)
col_hths <- run_ae_column(Y_hths)

cat("All 4 unit columns computed.\n")
## All 4 unit columns computed.

Results

build_unit_table(col_dol, col_cnt, col_ths, col_hths) %>%
  kbl(escape = FALSE, align = c("l","c","c","c","c"),
      caption = "SUW reproduction — Treatment: morekids. Effect of childbearing on log family income, across four income-unit shifts.") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = TRUE, font_size = 13) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "Income unit shift" = 4)) %>%
  pack_rows("Panel A: 2SLS (HC1-robust)",            1, 1) %>%
  pack_rows("Panel B: Normalized kappa estimators",  2, 4) %>%
  pack_rows("Panel C: Unnormalized kappa estimators",5, 7) %>%
  row_spec(2:4, background = "#eef6ff") %>%
  row_spec(5:7, background = "#fffbf0") %>%
  footnote(general = "Each cell: estimate (SE below). Blue = normalized (translation invariant: all four unit columns match). Orange = unnormalized (shift with units). Stars: * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01.")

2. Double ML — PLR-IV & Wald-AIPW

set.seed(42); dml_ae <- dml_with_smoother(Y_dol, D, X_dml, Z, n_cf_folds = 5, tune.parameters = "all")

res_ae <- summary(dml_ae)
##            Estimate        SE       t         p    
## PLR       -0.377878  0.047674 -7.9263 3.156e-15 ***
## PLR-IV     0.131498  0.988019  0.1331    0.8941    
## AIPW-ATE  -0.379711  0.048529 -7.8245 7.007e-15 ***
## Wald-AIPW  0.446080  0.413906  1.0777    0.2812    
## AIPW-ATT  -0.373337  0.047917 -7.7914 9.064e-15 ***
## AIPW-ATU  -0.383133  0.051730 -7.4063 1.678e-13 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
extract_w <- function(dml_obj) {
  om <- get_outcome_weights(dml_obj)
  check_omega_rows(om)
  list(plriv    = as.vector(om$omega["PLR-IV",    ]),
       waldaipw = as.vector(om$omega["Wald-AIPW", ]))
}
w_ae <- extract_w(dml_ae)

cat("DML fit complete.\n")
## DML fit complete.

Estimates

DML — morekids, Y = log family income (dollars)
Estimator Estimate SE t p ΣωY = τ̂
PLR-IV 0.1315 0.9880 0.1331 0.8941 TRUE
Wald-AIPW 0.4461 0.4139 1.0777 0.2812 TRUE
Note:
ΣωY = τ̂: algebraic identity check (must be TRUE).

DML vs. Kappa — Point Estimates

kappa_ae <- kappa_analytic_se_all(Y_dol, Z, D, X_kappa)

cell <- function(coef, se) {
  if (is.na(coef)) return("—")
  if (is.na(se) || !is.finite(se) || se <= 0) return(sprintf("%.3f (NA)", coef))
  pval  <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(coef / se))
  stars <- ifelse(pval < 0.01, "***", ifelse(pval < 0.05, "**",
                  ifelse(pval < 0.10, "*", "")))
  sprintf("%.3f%s (%.3f)", coef, stars, se)
}

dml_cell <- function(res, est) {
  coef_val <- get_estimate(res, est)
  se_val   <- if ("SE" %in% colnames(res)) as.numeric(res[est, "SE"])
              else if ("Std. Error" %in% colnames(res)) as.numeric(res[est, "Std. Error"])
              else NA_real_
  cell(coef_val, se_val)
}

dml_rows <- rbind(
  data.frame(Estimator = "PLR-IV (DML)",
             `morekids` = dml_cell(res_ae, "PLR-IV"),
             check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
  data.frame(Estimator = "Wald-AIPW (DML)",
             `morekids` = dml_cell(res_ae, "Wald-AIPW"),
             check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
)

ests   <- c("tau_cb_u","tau_ml_u","tau_ml_a10","tau_ml_a","tau_ml_t","tau_ml_a0")
labels <- c("τ_cb_u","τ_ml_u","τ_ml_a10","τ_ml_a","τ_ml_t","τ_ml_a0")
kappa_rows <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(ests), function(i) {
  e <- ests[i]
  data.frame(
    Estimator = labels[i],
    `morekids` = cell(kappa_ae$estimates[e], kappa_ae$se[e]),
    check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

rbind(dml_rows, kappa_rows) %>%
  kbl(align = c("l","c"),
      caption = "DML vs. Kappa — Point Estimates (SE), Y = log family income (dollars)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = TRUE, font_size = 13) %>%
  pack_rows("Double ML", 1, 2) %>%
  pack_rows("Kappa — normalized", 3, 5) %>%
  pack_rows("Kappa — unnormalized", 6, 8) %>%
  row_spec(1:2, background = "#eef6ff") %>%
  row_spec(6:8, background = "#fffbf0") %>%
  footnote(general = "Each cell: estimate (SE). Blue = DML. Orange = unnormalized kappa. Stars: * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01.")

τ_cb_u 0.429 (0.769)
τ_ml_u 0.429 (0.769)
τ_ml_a10 0.474 (0.801)
Kappa — unnormalized

3. Learner Comparison — Linear / Ranger / XGBoost

make_dml_data <- function(D, X_dml) {
  df <- as.data.frame(X_dml)
  df$y <- as.numeric(Y_dol)
  df$d <- as.numeric(D)
  df$z <- as.numeric(Z)
  DoubleMLData$new(data = df, y_col = "y", d_cols = "d", z_cols = "z",
                   x_cols = colnames(X_dml))
}

dat_ae <- make_dml_data(D, X_dml)

cat("DoubleML data object built.\n")
## DoubleML data object built.

Estimates

Linear + Logistic

lm_ae <- fit_iivm(dat_ae, ml_lm(), Y_dol)
learner_estimate_table(lm_ae, "Wald-AIPW — Linear + Logistic")
Wald-AIPW — Linear + Logistic
Cell Estimate SE ΣωY = τ̂
morekids NA NA NA
Note:
ΣωY = τ̂: algebraic identity check (must be TRUE).

Ranger

rf_ae <- fit_iivm(dat_ae, ml_rf(), Y_dol)
learner_estimate_table(rf_ae, "Wald-AIPW — Ranger")
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d    0.5196     0.4618   1.125    0.261

ΣωY = τ̂: algebraic identity check (must be TRUE).

XGBoost

xgb_ae <- fit_iivm(dat_ae, ml_xgb(), Y_dol)
learner_estimate_table(xgb_ae, "Wald-AIPW — XGBoost")

##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)

Comparison Table

ext_cell <- function(coef, se) {
  if (is.na(coef) || !is.finite(coef)) return("—")
  if (is.na(se) || !is.finite(se) || se <= 0) return(sprintf("%.3f (NA)", coef))
  pval  <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(coef / se))
  stars <- ifelse(pval < 0.01, "***", ifelse(pval < 0.05, "**",
                  ifelse(pval < 0.10, "*", "")))
  sprintf("%.3f%s (%.3f)", coef, stars, se)
}

learner_row <- function(label, f) {
  data.frame(
    Estimator  = label,
    `morekids` = ext_cell(safe_coef(f), safe_se(f)),
    check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

kappa_ref_row <- function(label, key) {
  data.frame(
    Estimator  = label,
    `morekids` = ext_cell(kappa_ae$estimates[key], kappa_ae$se[key]),
    check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

rbind(
  learner_row("Wald-AIPW (linear+logit)", lm_ae),
  learner_row("Wald-AIPW (ranger)",       rf_ae),
  learner_row("Wald-AIPW (XGBoost)",      xgb_ae),
  kappa_ref_row("τ_cb_u (kappa, ref)",  "tau_cb_u"),
  kappa_ref_row("τ_ml_u (kappa, ref)",  "tau_ml_u")
) %>%
  kbl(align = c("l","c"),
      caption = "Wald-AIPW Learner Comparison — DoubleML vs. Kappa (Y = log family income, dollars)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = TRUE, font_size = 13) %>%
  pack_rows("DoubleML learners", 1, 3) %>%
  pack_rows("Kappa reference",   4, 5) %>%
  row_spec(1:3, background = "#eef6ff") %>%
  footnote(general = "Each cell: estimate (SE). Blue = DoubleML. All score='LATE', n_folds=5. Stars: * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01.")

## 
## 
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)  
## d    0.9440     0.4866    1.94   0.0524 .
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

4. Weight Diagnostics

Reading the tables. Sum_w ≈ 0 confirms translation invariance. ESS_mod = modified effective sample size (Σ|ωᵢ|)²/Σωᵢ² (Chattopadhyay & Zubizarreta 2023) — the headline number, valid under negative weights and bounded in [1, n]. The old Kish ESS = 1/Σωᵢ² is shown alongside for reference but is unreliable for signed weights. With a complier share near 5%, this design produces concentrated weights and a low effective sample size — the modified ESS is the honest measure of usable mass.

DML vs. Kappa

style_dml_diag(dml_diag_cell(w_ae, kw_ae), "Weight Diagnostics — DML vs. Kappa | morekids")
Weight Diagnostics — DML vs. Kappa | morekids
Estimator Sum_w ESS ESS_mod Pct_neg Max_abs_w
PLR-IV (DML) 0.0000000 2 2006 49.8 0.055201
Wald-AIPW (DML) 0.0000000 3 2991 49.8 0.013690
τ_cb_u (kappa) 0.0000000 3 2989 49.8 0.013037
τ_ml_u (kappa) 0.0000000 3 2989 49.8 0.013043
τ_ml_a10 (kappa) 0.0000000 2 2982 49.8 0.013888
τ_ml_a (kappa) 0.0992064 3 2989 49.8 0.013198
τ_ml_t/a1 (kappa) 0.0945271 3 2989 49.8 0.012576
τ_ml_a0 (kappa) 0.1043953 2 2989 49.8 0.013888
Note:
Blue = DML. Sum_w ≈ 0 ⟺ translation invariant. ESS_mod = (Σ|ω|)²/Σω² (report this); ESS = 1/Σω² (reference only).

Learners vs. Kappa

style_ext_diag(ext_diag_block(kw_ae, lm_ae$w, rf_ae$w, xgb_ae$w),
               "Weight Diagnostics — Learners | morekids")
Weight Diagnostics — Learners | morekids
Estimator Sum_w ESS ESS_mod Pct_neg Max_abs_w
Wald-AIPW (linear+logit) NA NA NA NA NA
Wald-AIPW (ranger) 0.0000000 3 2987 49.8 0.017949
Wald-AIPW (XGBoost) 0.0012029 2 2552 49.7 0.053952
τ_cb_u (kappa, CBPS) 0.0000000 3 2989 49.8 0.013037
τ_ml_u (kappa, MLE) 0.0000000 3 2989 49.8 0.013043
τ_ml_a10 (kappa, MLE) 0.0000000 2 2982 49.8 0.013888
τ_ml_a (kappa, unnorm) 0.0992064 3 2989 49.8 0.013198
τ_ml_t (kappa, unnorm) 0.0945271 3 2989 49.8 0.012576
τ_ml_a0 (kappa, unnorm) 0.1043953 2 2989 49.8 0.013888
Note:
Blue = DoubleML learners. ESS_mod = (Σ|ω|)²/Σω² (report this); ESS = 1/Σω² (reference only). NA rows = learner produced no weights.

Weight-Statistics Bridge — RQ1

What this is. For every estimator, weight_bridge() reports the outcome weights Overall / Treated / Control (control sign-flipped, matching Knaus’s convention), putting the two diagnostic vocabularies side by side: his six summary() statistics (Min, Max, %Neg, Σ of the largest 10%, Σω, Σ|ω|) next to the two effective-sample-size measures (Kish vs. modified). The headline is ESS mod. The Knaus block is computed in plain R, so the kappa estimators get his statistics too, which his package never produces for them.

ESS overview (Kish vs. modified)

ess_overview_df %>%
  kbl(caption = "Effective sample size, Overall scope — Kish (1/Σω²) vs. modified ((Σ|ω|)²/Σω²)",
      col.names = c("Estimator","ESS Kish","ESS mod"),
      align = c("l","r","r")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = TRUE, font_size = 13) %>%
  column_spec(3, bold = TRUE, background = "#eef6ff") %>%
  footnote(general = "Where Kish collapses toward 1, the modified ESS recovers the genuine usable weight mass. Report the modified column.")
Effective sample size, Overall scope — Kish (1/Σω²) vs. modified ((Σ|ω|)²/Σω²)
Estimator ESS Kish ESS mod
PLR-IV (DML) 2 2006
Wald-AIPW (DML) 3 2991
Ranger 3 2987
XGBoost 2 2552
τ_cb_u (kappa) 3 2989
τ_ml_u (kappa) 3 2989
τ_ml_t (kappa) 3 2989
Note:
Where Kish collapses toward 1, the modified ESS recovers the genuine usable weight mass. Report the modified column. 
ggplot(ess_overview_df, aes(y = Estimator)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = ESS_kish, xend = ESS_mod, yend = Estimator),
               colour = "#bdbdbd", linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_point(aes(x = ESS_kish, colour = "Kish"),     size = 2.6) +
  geom_point(aes(x = ESS_mod,  colour = "Modified"), size = 2.6) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Kish" = "#9e9e9e", "Modified" = "#2c7bb6"), name = NULL) +
  labs(title = "Effective sample size: Kish → modified",
       x = "Effective sample size", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "top", panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

Full bridge table

bridge_table(bridge_w, D, "Weight-statistics bridge — morekids")

Control -0.0123121 0.0125755 48.1 2.239937 0.9054729 21.30080 4 1945
Note:
Control rows use Knaus’s sign-flipped −ω convention. ESS mod = (Σ|ω|)²/Σω² is the number to report; ESS Kish = 1/Σω² is reference only.

5. Love Plots

DML vs. Kappa (all 8 estimators)

grobs <- Filter(Negate(is.null), list(
  maybe_love("PLR-IV (DML)",     w_ae$plriv,       D, X_dml),
  maybe_love("Wald-AIPW (DML)",  w_ae$waldaipw,    D, X_dml),
  maybe_love("τ_cb_u (CBPS)",    kw_ae$tau_cb_u,   D, X_dml),
  maybe_love("τ_ml_u",           kw_ae$tau_ml_u,   D, X_dml),
  maybe_love("τ_ml_a10",         kw_ae$tau_ml_a10, D, X_dml),
  maybe_love("τ_ml_a (unnorm)",  kw_ae$tau_ml_a,   D, X_dml),
  maybe_love("τ_ml_t (unnorm)",  kw_ae$tau_ml_a1,  D, X_dml),
  maybe_love("τ_ml_a0 (unnorm)", kw_ae$tau_ml_a0,  D, X_dml)
))
grid.arrange(grobs = grobs, nrow = 2, top = "Love Plots — morekids: DML vs. Kappa")

Learners vs. Kappa (3 learners + 2 kappa)

grobs <- Filter(Negate(is.null), list(
  maybe_love("Linear+Logit", lm_ae$w,        D, X_dml),
  maybe_love("Ranger",       rf_ae$w,        D, X_dml),
  maybe_love("XGBoost",      xgb_ae$w,       D, X_dml),
  maybe_love("τ_cb_u",       kw_ae$tau_cb_u, D, X_dml),
  maybe_love("τ_ml_u",       kw_ae$tau_ml_u, D, X_dml)
))
grid.arrange(grobs = grobs, nrow = 1, top = "Love Plots — DoubleML learners vs. kappa | morekids")
